title: AST Theft in the 3rd Degree: Ketchikan [AK25056907]
summary: On 06/12/2025 at about 2132 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of a Theft at a residence on Higgins Spur road. Investigation revealed that a person(s) entered an unlocked residence and stole a battery supply charger from inside the residence. Case is under investigation. Anyone with information pertaining to the Theft or any criminal activity is asked to call the Alaska State Troopers at 225-5118. Callers can remain anonymous.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: a residence on Higgins Spur road

summary: The Powers family of Wrangell poses at the harbor. (Photo courtesy of Dan Powers) People across the country will celebrate Father’s Day on June 15. Listen to the story: Dan Powers and his wife, along with their eight kids, live in Wrangell, but some of them are currently in Petersburg, on a different island, at the boat harbor. title: June 2025 Newsletter
summary: Thank you to Newport | Budget Concerns | Executive Order 14276 | U.S. Coast Guard Announcement | Observer Program Annual Report | BSAI Crab Specs | Pelagic Trawl | Crab Arbitration and IFQ/ IPQ Withdrawal | AFA Program Review Report | Central GOA Rockfish Program Review Report | Central GOA Rockfish Program Review Report | Staff Tasking | Appointments | Upcoming Meetings Thank you to Newport Boat tour during the pelagic trawl gear demonstration. The North Pacific Fishery Management Council would like to extend its thanks to the many residents, businesses, and organizations that helped make the recent meeting in Newport, Oregon a success: with strong public participation, a warm welcome, and community hospitality. Newport is also within the traditional lands of the Yaquina ( Yaqo’n ) and Alsea ( Wusi’n ) Tribes, whose tribal descendants are represented by the Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians. The Council honors the Indigenous Peoples who have stewarded the land across generations a...

title: SIWO Forecast – June 13, 2025
summary: Assessment of Current Ice Conditions Relevant to Distribution and Access of Walrus Click the name of each community below to view more frequently updated and detailed information from the National Weather Service. Synopsis : Generally wet and rainy conditions to start off the period with the greatest rain chances for Nome occurring Friday through Saturday morning. St. Lawrence Island can expect to see rain starting Friday afternoon through Saturday evening. Potentially scattered showers may linger across the West Coast through Sunday night, but rain chances diminish afterwards. Temperatures will be in the 50s and 60s for the Seward Peninsula through the week, with St. Lawrence Island seeing slightly lower temperatures around the low 40s. The strongest winds will be out of the south/southeast between 20–35 mph (17–30 kts) and occur over St. Lawrence Island and the Bering Strait Saturday morning through Sunday morning. Near St. Lawrence Island Between Iwoorigan Camp and Savoonga, only ve...

title: AST DUI: Fairbanks [AK25056942]
summary: On June 12th, 2025, at approximately 2245 hours, Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop of Mia Van Hoy (21F of Fairbanks) on Peger Rd. in Fairbanks. Troopers observed an open, half-drank,1.75L container of Buzzball in the backseat. Additionally, a passerby stated they had been behind Mia and observed her driving dangerously (to include running multiple red lights). Following an investigation Mia was found to be impaired by alcohol at three times the legal limit. Mia was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center without further issue and charged with DUI.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Special Edition of Kayla’s Corner
summary: Extending our well wishes and congratulations to newlywed district residents Kayla Epstein and Gordan Glaser. The post Special Edition of Kayla’s Corner appeared first on Alaska Senate Majority .

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Palmer [AK19070514]
summary: On 6/13/2025 AST came into contact with Renee Markee in Palmer. She was found to have a warrant for her arrest for Failure to Appear on a DUI. She was arrested and remanded to the Mat-Su Pre-Trial facility and held on $500 bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
